folio.label: 161r
folio.l:
wounded his dougúter , witú fyue mortal woundes , in fyue
places sondry ,

this is to seyn , in hire feet , in hire handes , in
hire Erys , in hire Nose , and in hire moutú , and leften hire for
ded , and wenten a wey

¢ Whan Melibeus , retorned was in to
his hous , and sey al this meschief , he like a mad man , rendy~ge
hise clothes , gan to wepe & crye

¢ Prudence his wyf , as ferfortú
as she dorste , bisougúte hi~ , of his wepyngö for to stynte ,

but nat
for thy , he gan to crie and wepyn , euäe lengere the more , ,

This
noble wyf Prudence , remembred hire vp on the sentence of Ouy-
de in his book , that cleped is , the remedie of loue , where as he
seitú ,

he is a fool that destourbetú the moder to wepe in the detú
of hire child , til she haue wept hire fille , as for a certeyn ty-
me ,

and thanne shal man don his diligence , w amyable wordes
hire to reconforte , and preye hire , of hire wepyng for to stynte ,

for whicú resou~ , this noble wyf Prudence , suffred hire hous-
bond for to wepe and crye , as for a certeyn space ,

And whan
she say hire tyme , she seide to hi~ in this wyse , Allas my
lord quod she , why make ©e ©oure self , for to be like a fool ,

for
Sothe , it ap¹tenetú nat to a wys man , to maken swicú a sorwe ,
©oure dougúter , w the grace of god , shal warisshe and escape ,

And al were it so , that she rigút now were ded , ©e ne ougút
nat , as for hire detú , ©oure self to destroye ,

Senec seitú , the
wyse man shal nat take to gret discomfort , for the detú of hise
children ,

but certes he shulde suffren it in pacience , as wel ,
as he abidetú the detú , of his owene ¸pre ¹sone

¢ This Meli-
beus answered a noon & seide , what man q¶ he shulde of his
wepyngö stynte , that hatú so gret a cause for to wepe

¢ Ihu~
Crist oure lord , hi~ self wepte , for the detú of lazarus his frend

Prudence answered , Certes wel I wot attempree wepyngö is
no thyngö defended to hi~ that sorweful is , amonges folk in
sorwe , but it is rathere gunted hi~ to wepe

¢ The Apostle
Poule , vn to the Romayns wrytetú , Man shal reioise , w he~
that maken ioye , And wepen , witú swicú folk as wepyn ,

But thougú attempre wepyngö , be I gunted , outgeous we-
pyngö , certes is defended ,

Mesure of wepyng , shulde be conside-
red , after the loore that techetú vs Senek ,

whan that thy frend
is ded quod he , lat nat thyne eyen to moiste ben of teeres , ne
to meche drye , al thougú the teeris , come to thyne eyen , lat
hem nat falle ;

And whan thow hast for gon thy frend , do dili-
gence , to gete a geyn a nother frend , and this is more wys-
dom , than for to wepe , for thy frend , whicú that æè hast lorn~ ,
for there Inne is no bote ,
And æäfore if ©e gouäne ©ow by sapi-
